subject: Re: Question about tilt series dose in warp
body:
Hi Alister,
wow, in that case, it makes sense, indeed like you said the difference 
would be  negligible.

Thank you for answering the question! Saved me from a lot of works!

Yours sincerely,
Linhua Tai

On Friday, March 27, 2020 at 7:32:04 PM UTC+8, Alister Burt wrote:
>
> Hi Linhua,
>
> Just a guess but I assume if the pre-exposure does is 0 e/A2 and post 
> exposure is 3 e/A2 then the average dose is 1.5 e/A2 and thus the dose 
> filtering is applied according to this? This would mean that maybe the 
> first few frames are over filtered and the last few are under filtered but 
> I imagine the final difference is very small!
>
> Best,
>
> Alister
>
> On 27 Mar 2020, at 12:10, Linhua Tai <888...@gmail.com <javascript:>> 
> wrote:
>
> ﻿
>
> Dear all,
> Hi, I'm using a dose symmetric scheme, and after import imod aligned tilt 
> series back to warp, and set the dose to 3 e/A, which is the dose at 0 
> degree, I found out in fourier space tab, the dose at 0 degree is 1.5e and 
> increase by 4.5, 7.5, 10.5 etc.
> The order is right for my tilt scheme, but the dose isn't right.
>
> So my question is, what does the dose value stand for? Is it pre-exposure 
> dose, or post-exposure dose?
>
> Should I need to change the value in *.tomostar files to match the real 
> case?
>
> Thank you very much in advance!
>
> Yours sincerely,
> Linhua Tai
>
> --

> .
>
>
